description_long: This is a single-bladed axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain texture. The head of the axe is metallic, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end, both showing signs of wear and use. The blade is finished in a dark metallic gray, while the poll and upper part of the head have a reddish-brown patina, possibly from oxidation or paint. The handle is ergonomically shaped, tapering towards the end for a secure grip, and appears to be made from a dense hardwood. The mounting of the blade to the handle is traditional, with the head fitted over the top of the handle and likely wedged in place. No visible markings, engravings, or labels are present on either the blade or the handle.
description: A robust, single-bladed axe with a curved wooden handle and a dark metallic head, featuring a broad blade and a reddish-brown patina.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped metallic blade with a reddish-brown patina.
description_view_1: Narrow end-on view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the rounded end of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same curved handle and blade, with the poll and blade edge clearly visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the thickness of the blade and the alignment of the handle with the head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, ergonomic taper, oval cross-section, and no added grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped blade with a straight edge, flat poll, and no beard; features a reddish-brown patina.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the head fitted over the top of the handle.
